title: Reading Comprehension Questions
tagline: Cite Evidence | Support Claims | Analyze Text
objectives:
• Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says
• Use evidence to make inferences and draw conclusions from text
directions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer for questions 1-5. Write your response for questions 6-10.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text best supports the main idea?

options.A: The first sentence of paragraph two
options.B: The last sentence of paragraph one
options.C: The middle sentence of paragraph three

questions.answer: B - The last sentence of paragraph one

questions.question: What evidence shows the author's opinion about the topic?
Which detail supports the inference that change occurred?
What textual evidence proves the claim made in paragraph two?
Which phrase best supports the author's conclusion?
Identify two pieces of evidence that support the author's main argument.
questions.answer: Students should identify specific quotes or facts from the text that directly support the central claim made by the author.

questions.question: What inference can you make based on the evidence in paragraph three?
questions.answer: Students should state a logical conclusion drawn from textual details and explain how the evidence leads to that inference.

questions.question: How does the evidence in the text support or contradict your prior knowledge?
questions.answer: Students should compare textual evidence to what they already knew and explain whether the text confirms or challenges their understanding.

questions.question: Analyze how the author uses evidence throughout the text to build their argument.
questions.answer: Students should examine the progression of evidence, explaining how different types of support work together to strengthen the author's position.

questions.question: Evaluate the strength of the evidence presented and explain whether it convincingly supports the main claim.
questions.answer: Students should assess the quality and relevance of textual evidence, determining if it adequately proves the author's main argument.
